topic: VS Code Autopilot Mode: Your Agent Never Stops Until the Job Is Done
hook: VS Code 1.111 just gave your AI agent a turbo button
audience: Developers using GitHub Copilot in VS Code who want to maximize AI productivity
keyTakeaway: Autopilot mode lets your agent run autonomously until task completion, but understanding the three permission levels is crucial for safe usage
talkingPoints: VS Code 1.111 dropped March 9th with Autopilot mode - here's what it actually does
Walk through the three permission levels: Default Approvals, Bypass Approvals, and Autopilot
Demo: Watch the agent iterate autonomously on a real task using task_complete signaling
The security warning you need to understand before enabling Bypass or Autopilot
My recommendation: When to use each permission level based on task type
trendContext: VS Code 1.111 released March 9, 2026 (4 days ago) with Autopilot mode as headline feature - this is the first weekly Stable release in VS Code's new release cadence
